expectations.text: Response contains a fenced, paste-ready prompt block
expectations.evidence: A single ```text ... ``` fenced block under **Pushed Prompt** containing the full copyable prompt, delimited from surrounding prose.

expectations.text: Response does not perform the underlying task itself
expectations.evidence: Whole response instructs Claude Code (explore repo, present menu, implement after confirm); it does not add auth itself.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt includes an explicit rule for when to ask vs when to proceed with assumptions
expectations.evidence: 'Ask clarifying questions only when my answer would materially change the data model, UX flow, or security posture. Otherwise make a conservative assumption, state it briefly, and continue.'

expectations.text: Pushed prompt routes discovery to the agent's own tools (inspect repo/files/deps) rather than asking the user everything or guessing
expectations.evidence: 'Explore the repo before proposing anything. Find: the entry point / app factory, whether this is a server-rendered app... the ORM and migration setup... any existing User model...'

expectations.text: Response contains no actual implementation code (no Python/Flask code that performs auth)
expectations.evidence: No Python/Flask code anywhere; only natural-language instructions and a security baseline listing (argon2/bcrypt, CSRF, cookie flags).

expectations.text: Pushed prompt names concrete auth security specifics (e.g., password hashing algorithm, session/cookie hardening, CSRF) rather than only generic 'secure' language
expectations.evidence: 'hash passwords with argon2 or bcrypt... set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite on the session cookie; add CSRF protection to every form (Flask-WTF)... same generic error... rate-limit failed logins...'

expectations.text: Pushed prompt enumerates key auth decision forks as a concrete menu (e.g., auth method, credential/storage model, protected scope, definition of done) with options and a recommendation request, and asks which are in scope now vs deferred
expectations.evidence: 'give me a decision menu... For each fork: list the realistic options, recommend the one that fits... 1. Auth approach... 2. Feature scope... 3. User storage...' then 'ask me which items are in scope now versus deferred.'

expectations.text: Response contains a fenced, paste-ready prompt block
expectations.evidence: A single ```text ... ``` fenced block under **Pushed Prompt** with the copyable prompt.

expectations.text: Response does not perform the underlying task itself
expectations.evidence: It instructs the agent to 'Find and fix the typo in my README' and scan the file; it does not itself edit any README.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt is calibrated small: roughly 8 lines or fewer inside the fenced block
expectations.evidence: The fenced block is a single compact paragraph (one logical line of directives), well under the 8-line ceiling.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt does NOT contain a full template apparatus (Context/Goal/Constraints/Unknowns/Working method/Quality bar all spelled out)
expectations.evidence: No labeled sections at all — one flowing paragraph, and the surrounding prose explicitly says 'Small, clear task — no template.'

expectations.text: Pushed prompt is written in imperative voice: verb-first commands, not descriptive narration
expectations.evidence: Verb-first throughout: 'Find and fix... Scan the whole file... Leave code blocks... alone... Don't rewrite... End with a short before -> after list.'

expectations.text: Response contains a fenced, paste-ready prompt block
expectations.evidence: A single ```text ... ``` fenced block under **Pushed Prompt** with the full copyable prompt.

expectations.text: Response does not perform the underlying task itself
expectations.evidence: The response instructs the receiving model ('Teach me quantum computing from scratch...'); it does not carry out the teaching itself.

expectations.text: Response does not itself explain quantum computing (no substantive physics explanation outside the prompt block)
expectations.evidence: Prose outside the block is meta only (e.g. Diagnosis notes explainers 'repeat myths'); no substantive explanation of qubits/superposition is delivered outside the fenced prompt.

expectations.text: If the pushed prompt asks the target to research/search, it bounds it: finite source count, rounds, or an explicit stop condition
expectations.evidence: Search is scoped and gated, not open-ended: 'If you can search the web, verify current hardware milestones before citing them; if not, date-stamp those claims' — 'before citing' is the stop condition and scope is limited to verifying milestones.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt specifies a concrete default audience/level (stated as a correctable assumption) rather than only a fill-in placeholder or an upfront question
expectations.evidence: 'Assume I'm a curious adult with no physics background and rusty math — say so up front and correct course if I tell you otherwise, but don't wait to ask: start explaining.'

expectations.text: Response contains a fenced, paste-ready prompt block

expectations.text: Response does not perform the underlying task itself
expectations.evidence: The response instructs Cursor to explore, propose directions, and implement; it does not redesign the page itself.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt requests 2-4 meaningfully different design directions/options before full implementation
expectations.evidence: 'propose 3 meaningfully different visual directions... Build each as the redesigned hero plus one content section... on temporary preview routes... Wait for my pick before touching the production page.'

expectations.text: Response contains no actual HTML/CSS implementation
expectations.evidence: No HTML/CSS code; frameworks (Tailwind, CSS modules) are named only as things to detect during exploration.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt includes an ask-vs-proceed or assumption-logging rule
expectations.evidence: 'Ask me only when the answer would change the design direction or scope... otherwise make a conservative assumption, state it, and continue.' Plus explicit '(Assumption — tell me if...)' logging.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt includes a blindspot instruction (surface anything that would change the work) in addition to requesting design directions
expectations.evidence: 'do a quick blindspot pass: surface anything that would change the work — SEO meta, analytics events, or form handlers wired into the current markup that a redesign could silently break; missing assets; brand constraints... List what you'll preserve.' — present alongside the 3-directions request.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt names concrete auth security specifics (e.g., password hashing algorithm, session/cookie hardening, CSRF) rather than only generic 'secure' language
expectations.evidence: 'Hash with werkzeug.security or bcrypt... CSRF protection on every form; Session cookies: HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure flag in production... open-redirect' validation.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt enumerates key auth decision forks as a concrete menu (e.g., auth method, credential/storage model, protected scope, definition of done) with options and a recommendation request, and asks which are in scope now vs deferred
expectations.evidence: The prompt pre-decides the fork ('Add session-based user authentication... using Flask-Login') and gives a fixed 'Out of scope' list; it does not present forks as an options menu with a recommendation request or ask which are in scope now vs deferred.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt specifies a concrete default audience/level (stated as a correctable assumption) rather than only a fill-in placeholder or an upfront question

expectations.text: Response contains a fenced, paste-ready prompt block
expectations.evidence: A single ``` fenced block holds the full ready-to-paste Cursor prompt.

expectations.text: Response does not perform the underlying task itself
expectations.evidence: Despite detailed design specs, it is a prompt for Cursor to execute (plan then implement); the response writes no page itself.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt requests 2-4 meaningfully different design directions/options before full implementation
expectations.evidence: It commits to one direction ('modern and clean, in the spirit of Linear, Vercel, and Stripe') with fixed specs; the only checkpoint is 'show me a short plan... and wait for my approval' — a single-direction plan, not 2-4 options.

expectations.text: Response contains no actual HTML/CSS implementation
expectations.evidence: All specs are prose directives (e.g. '48-72px', 'border radius... 8px', '4/8px spacing scale'); no markup or CSS rules are written.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt includes an ask-vs-proceed or assumption-logging rule
expectations.evidence: No rule governing when to ask vs proceed on assumptions; the plan-then-approve gate is a review checkpoint, and 'placeholders marked TODO' only covers fabricated social proof, not a general assumption-logging rule.

expectations.text: Pushed prompt includes a blindspot instruction (surface anything that would change the work) in addition to requesting design directions
expectations.evidence: It only constrains preservation ('Don't touch routing, forms, analytics') without instructing Cursor to proactively surface anything that would change the work; and it does not request multiple design directions.
